note: Fictional Rediem demo dossier. Public prospecting estimates are confidence-scored and should not be treated as exact customer metrics.

brandProfile.targetCustomer: Wellness-minded consumers who buy functional drinks in retail and online

retailSignals.evidence: Store locator and retailer badges visible on sample pages

brandProfile.missionDrivenAngle: Better-for-you soda alternative with gut health education

explanation: Visible reviews, UGC, subscriptions, and retail presence suggest real participation potential.
The loyalty motion appears points-heavy, so earned community behavior may not be fully captured.
Retail buyers appear disconnected from owned DTC profiles, making receipt upload a strong first loop.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Participation Capture Gap
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Visible participation demand is estimated at 100 while owned participation capture is estimated at 73. This is a public-signal estimate, not a measured customer conversion gap.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: Customers appear to be participating across reviews, social, retail, or subscriptions, but the owned capture path looks thinner than the participation surface. Rediem can turn those behaviors into verified member actions and rewards.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Retail-to-Community Bridge Index
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Retail bridge readiness is estimated from retail presence (90), owned commerce surface (66), participation proof (58), and receipt bridge evidence (40). No retail volume is inferred.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: Retail appears to create reach, but the public journey may not fully pull buyers into owned community. Rediem can use receipt verification to connect retail purchases to member profiles, rewards, and follow-on DTC actions.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Mission-to-Action Ratio
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Mission narrative intensity is estimated at 81; visible mission-linked action depth is estimated at 62. The score rises when the mission is visible but customer action paths are weaker.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: The brand has visible mission or identity language, but the public customer journey gives people limited ways to act on it. Rediem can turn the mission into verified challenges, stories, preferences, and rewards.

gtmDiagnostics.label: UGC Verification Gap
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Public UGC energy is estimated at 100; verified member or purchase capture is estimated at 29. Use this as a verification opportunity, not a claim about actual UGC volume.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: The brand has visible social or creator energy, but public evidence does not clearly show that content being verified against customer identity. Rediem can make UGC a verified participation loop.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Discount Dependence Ratio
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Visible discount pressure is estimated at 18; participation reward depth is estimated at 71. This does not infer margin, CAC, promo spend, or actual retention performance.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: The public retention motion appears more discount-led than participation-led. Rediem can keep incentives in the journey while moving more value behind verified reviews, referrals, preferences, status, and access.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Zero-Party Data Depth
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Preference capture breadth is estimated at 14, specificity at 20, and reward or personalization connection at 44. The score reflects visible declared-data depth, not internal data storage.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: The brand has a natural preference story. Rediem can make preference capture a rewarded community action and use declared intent to personalize participation, rewards, referrals, and subscriptions.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Product Drop Participation Score
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Drop attention is estimated at 100; owned signup at 54; participation mechanics at 60; post-drop loop at 35. No sell-through, waitlist size, or launch revenue is inferred.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: Launch attention appears available. Rediem can make each product drop a participation campaign by rewarding join, share, review, referral, vote, UGC, and preference actions.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Stack Fragmentation Index
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: 4 public participation stack categories were detected. The score reflects apparent fragmentation from public detections and does not claim exact internal architecture.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: The stack appears to capture useful behaviors in separate places: reviews, loyalty, subscriptions, messages, or referrals. Rediem can sit across those surfaces as the participation layer that makes the customer profile usable.

gtmDiagnostics.label: Owned Community Conversion Score
gtmDiagnostics.explanation: Public community demand is estimated at 100 while owned conversion surface is estimated at 73. The score reflects visible conversion readiness, not actual conversion rate.
gtmDiagnostics.outboundAngle: The brand has public community energy, but the owned conversion path looks thinner than demand. Rediem can turn that attention into member profiles, verified actions, and repeatable participation.

primaryGtmDiagnosis.label: UGC Verification Gap
primaryGtmDiagnosis.outboundAngle: The brand has visible social or creator energy, but public evidence does not clearly show that content being verified against customer identity. Rediem can make UGC a verified participation loop.

flywheelLeaks.description: Rewards language centers on points and VIP tiers, with limited evidence of reviews, referrals, UGC, or zero-party data connected to loyalty.
flywheelLeaks.recommendedFix: Audit the points program and migrate high-intent customers into participation-based rewards.

flywheelLeaks.description: Retail presence is visible, but the sample evidence does not show a receipt upload or retail-to-owned-profile loop.
flywheelLeaks.recommendedFix: Launch a receipt upload challenge to turn retail buyers into owned community profiles.

activationIdeas.title: Turn retail buyers into owned community profiles
activationIdeas.targetBehavior: Upload a retail receipt, join the community profile, and receive a personalized DTC reward
activationIdeas.whyItFits: The brand has retail presence, reviews, UGC, and subscriptions, but no visible retail-to-owned loop.
activationIdeas.rediemValuePropAngle: Connect retail demand to owned customer profiles and follow-on subscriptions.
activationIdeas.suggestedOutboundOneLiner: Your retail and review footprint suggests customers are already participating; Rediem can help convert those offline buyers into owned profiles and repeat DTC loops.

activationIdeas.title: Move points and VIP tiers into participation-based rewards
activationIdeas.targetBehavior: Reward reviews, referrals, UGC, preference completion, and subscription milestones instead of only purchases
activationIdeas.whyItFits: The sample loyalty page emphasizes points and VIP tiers while reviews and UGC appear separate.
activationIdeas.rediemValuePropAngle: Shift loyalty from transaction subsidy to verified community participation.
activationIdeas.suggestedOutboundOneLiner: Your VIP program already has the scaffolding; Rediem can help make the rewards loop about verified participation, not just points.

economicBuyers.title: VP Marketing
economicBuyers.angle: Revenue impact, brand community differentiation, and retention strategy

operatorBuyers.title: Director of Retention
operatorBuyers.angle: Lifecycle, loyalty migration, subscription retention, and campaign execution

technicalBuyers.title: Head of Ecommerce
technicalBuyers.angle: Commerce stack, integrations, data flows, and implementation risk

influencers.title: Community Manager
influencers.angle: UGC, creator participation, community programming, and social proof

recommendedFirstContact.title: Director of Retention
recommendedFirstContact.reason: Most directly owns retention, lifecycle, loyalty, and repeat-purchase loops.

outboundAngles.angleTitle: Retail participation is not yet owned
outboundAngles.whyNow: Retail presence and review activity are visible, but receipt-to-profile capture is not.
outboundAngles.painHypothesis: Retail buyers are creating demand and proof, but their participation is not turning into owned DTC profiles.
outboundAngles.suggestedOneLiner: Rediem can help convert retail buyers into owned community members through receipt upload, rewards, and follow-on subscription loops.

outboundAngles.angleTitle: Points loyalty is leaving community value uncaptured
outboundAngles.whyNow: The rewards page emphasizes points and VIP tiers while reviews and UGC appear separate.
outboundAngles.painHypothesis: The brand may be subsidizing purchases without capturing verified participation.
outboundAngles.suggestedOneLiner: Rediem can help turn points/VIP loyalty into participation-based rewards for reviews, referrals, UGC, and preferences.

n8nExport.sequencerEligibilityReason: Sample dossier does not include a verified email.

evidence.rawExcerpt: Earn points and climb VIP tiers with every purchase.
Customer reviews and product ratings are displayed on the sample review page.
Find us at Target, Whole Foods, and Sprouts.
Join our creator community and share your daily ritual.

evidence.value: better-for-you soda alternative with gut health education
evidence.rawExcerpt: Better-for-you soda alternative with gut health education and recyclable packaging.

evidence.value: limited seasonal flavor drop early access
evidence.rawExcerpt: New limited seasonal flavor drop with early access for VIP members.
